settings.text: What we do, and what it costs.
Pricing ranges by length, condition, and stylist level. For color work, a consultation comes first — the menus below cover our core services.

settings.name: Women's cut
settings.description: Consultation, shampoo, precision cut, style and finish.

settings.name: Men's cut
settings.description: Classic, taper, fade, or editorial. Includes neck shape-up.

settings.name: Kids' cut
settings.description: 12 and under. Gentle, patient, no rushing.

settings.name: Fringe trim
settings.description: Between appointments. Walk-in when we have space.

settings.title: Color, tone, and dimension.
settings.description: Balayage and color correction require a consultation before booking.

settings.name: Single-process color
settings.description: All-over color or root touch-up. Gloss finish included.

settings.name: Gloss & tone
settings.description: Refresh color between appointments. Thirty minutes.

settings.name: Highlights
settings.description: Foil work, partial or full. Length and thickness affect final price.

settings.name: Balayage
settings.description: Hand-painted color. Consultation required before first booking.

settings.name: Olaplex restoration
settings.description: Bond-building treatment for damaged or over-processed hair.

settings.name: Keratin smoothing
settings.description: Frizz reduction and manageability. Lasts three to five months.

settings.name: Deep conditioning
settings.description: Added on to any service, or booked as a standalone.

settings.name: Scalp treatment
settings.description: Clarifying and balancing. Good for product buildup or dry conditions.

settings.title: The quiet parts of a good appointment.
Full consultation
settings.description: Every visit starts with your stylist looking at what your hair is actually doing.

settings.title: Shampoo & scalp
settings.description: Included with every cut or color.

settings.title: Precision finish
settings.description: Dry cut refinements after the blow-out.

settings.title: Styling
settings.description: Finished the way you can redo at home.

settings.title: Ammonia-free options
settings.description: Available for every color service on request.

settings.title: Gentle on sensitive scalps
settings.description: Speak up at consultation; we adjust.

settings.title: Gloss finish
settings.description: Free with every color appointment.

settings.title: Honest timing
settings.description: We text a real ETA, not a guess.

settings.title: Why ranges, and what shifts the price.
Not sure what to book?
settings.text: Tell us your hair length, last appointment, and what you're thinking. We'll help you pick the right service before you book.

settings.question: Why isn't there a single price for my cut?
settings.answer: Length, density, and condition all affect how long a cut takes. The range reflects that range honestly — you'll know the final number before we start.

settings.question: What does "from" mean on a price?
settings.answer: It means that's the starting price for the shortest or simplest version of the service. Longer, thicker, or more-damaged hair takes longer to work with, and we price honestly for that.

settings.question: Why is a consultation required for color?
settings.answer: Because color done right starts with seeing your hair in person, understanding its history, and agreeing on an outcome. Same-day bookings for balayage or color correction almost always end badly.

settings.question: Do you charge more for long hair?
settings.answer: Yes — long hair takes more time to wash, cut, and finish, and more product to treat. The range on every service accounts for length.

settings.question: Same price for men's and women's cuts?
settings.answer: We price by time and technique, not gender. A short precision bob and a men's scissor cut cost roughly the same. A long layered cut costs more than either, regardless of who's in the chair.
